titleStmt.title: Translation of BL Add. Ch. 76749 (previously, BM Loans, 29/355)

p.seg: Robert of London, to all his own men, future as well as present, clerk and lay, French and English, greeting.

Be it known to all, future as well as present, that I, Robert of London, gave, conceded, and by this my charter made firm, to Walter of Berkeley my cousin, one ploughgate of land in Lessudden; and Plenmeller, my land of Tynedale; in return for his homage and service, in feu and heritage; for him and his heirs to hold of me and my heirs, free and quit, fully and honourably, in wood and in plain, in meadows and pastures, in waters and mills, rendering to me and my heirs annually two sore sparrowhawks by way of the service which pertains to me, reserving the king's service.

With these as witnesses: William son of Odard; William of Forscote; William of Conisbrough; Maurice de Reingny; Henry son of Jordan; William the clerk of Chausi; Gregory the parson; Roger the clerk, who composed this charter; Richard de Cameis; William de Tosny; Ralph Beleth; Maccus the provost; Robert Bochart; Osmund the servant; Walter son of Geoffrey; 'Paruum consilium' [i.e. 'Modest advice']; and Unwine.
